similar_questions.title: Divide Nodes Into the Maximum Number of Groups

discussion_posts.name: gflanary
discussion_posts.content: This is a straightforward problem, but the description is positively awful.\n\nIf they had an image with disjoint sets and especially if they had one with colorized groupings, it would be clear what the intent was.

discussion_posts.name: sasuke_uchiha
discussion_posts.content:
Hi, Can please someone help me explainning How the input
[[1,3], [0,2], [1,3], [0,2]] 
is working. I am not able to understand How does this input make this graph
0----1
|      |
|      |
3----2

discussion_posts.name: user8228j
discussion_posts.content: an hour and 15 minutes of my life gone.

discussion_posts.name: meow_power
discussion_posts.content:
Equivalent definitions of a bipartite graph:
1) There is no cycle of odd length

2) we can split the nodes of the graph
(vertex set of the graph) into 2 subsets so
that there is all the edges go from 1 subset
to the other subset.

3.The graph should be bi-colourable.

discussion_posts.name: Pankaj__123
discussion_posts.content: The good description can be -- color the graph with 2 colors such that no adjacent nodes have same color.

discussion_posts.name: sasukesharma
discussion_posts.content: Is this description so good that they locked the editorial \uD83D\uDE44

discussion_posts.name: mc_montecarlo
discussion_posts.content:
I have a doubt with the test-case:
[[4],[],[4],[4],[0,2,3]]

Above test-case represents a graph is isn't connected. But, the expected result of above is true. So my question is, how can a dis-connected graph be bi-partite?

discussion_posts.name: lukevastus
discussion_posts.content:
Apparently the graph should look like a triangle. 	
So why is the number 2 neither in graph[0] or in graph[1]?
The correct test case should be [[1, 2], [0, 2], [0, 1]]
